# Cron Builder - shadcn registry

> A cron expression builder for shadcn projects. Distributed as a shadcn custom registry block. Themed with your shadcn tokens.

This file is written for AI coding agents. Paste it alongside your own codebase to integrate or configure the component.

## Install

```bash
pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://angelocala94.github.io/cron-builder/r/cron-builder.json
```

Swap `pnpm` for `npm`, `yarn`, or `bun` if needed. Requires React 18+ and Tailwind v4 with the shadcn preset.

## Usage

```tsx
import * as React from 'react'
import { Cron } from '@/components/cron-builder'

export function Schedule() {
  const [value, setValue] = React.useState('30 5 * * 1-5')
  return <Cron value={value} setValue={setValue} />
}
```

Fully controlled: `value` in, updates via `setValue` or `onChange`.
The registry installs one component file plus three support files under `lib/cron-builder/`.

If you also need the current period, use `onChange` instead of `setValue`:

```tsx
import * as React from 'react'
import { Cron } from '@/components/cron-builder'

export function ScheduleWithPeriod() {
  const [value, setValue] = React.useState('30 5 * * 1-5')
  const [selectedPeriod, setSelectedPeriod] = React.useState('week')

  return (
    <Cron
      value={value}
      onChange={(nextValue, { selectedPeriod: nextPeriod }) => {
        setValue(nextValue)
        setSelectedPeriod(nextPeriod)
      }}
    />
  )
}
```

## Cron syntax

Five-field POSIX (`minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week`) with:

- ranges - `1-5`, `MON-FRI`
- lists - `1,15,30`
- steps - `*/5`, `0-30/10`
- wildcards - `*`
- weekday/month names - `MON`, `JAN` (parsed on input, emitted as numbers)
- Sunday accepts both `0` and `7` on input; the parser normalizes `7` to `0`
- shortcuts - `@yearly`, `@annually`, `@monthly`, `@weekly`, `@daily`, `@midnight`, `@hourly`, `@reboot`

Out-of-range values (e.g. minute `60`) fire `onError`.
`@reboot` is supported by the converter, and by the UI when you enable it through `shortcuts={true}` or by including `@reboot` in the shortcuts array. In reboot mode the builder collapses to the period selector plus the main Clear action.

## Props

### Core

- `value: string` - required. Current cron expression.
- `setValue?: (value: string) => void` - optional. Called on every edit when you only need the next cron string. Provide this or `onChange`.
- `onChange?: (value, { selectedPeriod }) => void` - optional. Alternative to `setValue`; also reports the current period. Provide this or `setValue`.
- `className?: string` - classes merged onto the root element.

### Selection

- `mode?: 'multiple' | 'single'` (default `'multiple'`) - dropdowns hold several values vs. one.
- `allowEmpty?: 'always' | 'never' | 'for-default-value'` (default `'for-default-value'`) - when wildcard (`*`) is allowed.
- `shortcuts?: boolean | ShortcutsType[]` (default `['@yearly', '@annually', '@monthly', '@weekly', '@daily', '@midnight', '@hourly']`) - which `@`-shortcuts are accepted as input for `value` (they are expanded internally) and whether `@reboot` appears as a period option. Pass `true` to accept every shortcut including `@reboot`, `false` to disable all shortcuts, or an array to restrict explicitly.
- `defaultPeriod?: PeriodType` (default `'day'`) - period chosen when `value` is empty, and tie-breaker for cron strings compatible with several periods (e.g. `10 10 * * *` fits day / week / month / year). Re-evaluated when the prop changes at runtime.
- `allowedPeriods?: PeriodType[]` - restrict selectable periods.
- `allowedDropdowns?: CronType[]` - restrict visible dropdowns.
- `allowClear?: boolean` (default `true`) - per-field clear button inside each field dropdown.
- `periodicityOnDoubleClick?: boolean` (default `true`) - double-click a value to build every-N schedules. When enabled, single-click selection waits briefly so the component can distinguish a real double click; when disabled, option clicks apply immediately.

### Display

- `clockFormat?: '24-hour-clock' | '12-hour-clock'` (default `'24-hour-clock'`) - with `'24-hour-clock'` hours and minutes are zero-padded (`05`, `09`, `17`); with `'12-hour-clock'` hours show `AM` / `PM` labels (`5AM`, `8PM`).
- `humanizeValue?: boolean` (default `false`) - render selected months/weekdays as labels instead of numbers.
- `humanizeLabels?: boolean` (default `true`) - friendly labels in option lists.
- `leadingZero?: boolean | ('month-days' | 'hours' | 'minutes')[]` (default `false`) - prefix numeric values with a leading zero. Note: hours and minutes are already padded when `clockFormat` is `'24-hour-clock'` (the default), so this prop is mostly useful for `month-days`.

### Clear button

- `clearButton?: boolean` (default `true`) - inline clear action.
- `clearButtonAction?: 'empty' | 'fill-with-every'` (default `'fill-with-every'`) - what the Clear button emits. `'empty'` emits an empty string (`''`); `'fill-with-every'` emits `* * * * *` (every field explicitly wildcarded).
- `clearButtonProps?: ButtonHTMLAttributes` - forwarded to the button element.

### State & validation

- `disabled?: boolean` (default `false`).
- `readOnly?: boolean` (default `false`) - locks the value without dimming.
- `displayError?: boolean` (default `true`) - built-in inline error state.
- `onError?: (error: CronError) => void` - fires with `{ type: 'invalid_cron', description }` when the current value can't be parsed, and `undefined` when valid again.

### Advanced

- `dropdownsConfig?: DropdownsConfig` - per-dropdown overrides. Keys: `period`, `months`, `month-days`, `week-days`, `hours`, `minutes`.
- `period` supports only `disabled` and `readOnly`.
- `months` / `week-days` support `humanizeLabels`, `humanizeValue`, `allowClear`, `allowEmpty`, `disabled`, `readOnly`, `periodicityOnDoubleClick`, `mode`, `filterOption`.
- `month-days` / `hours` / `minutes` support `leadingZero`, `allowClear`, `allowEmpty`, `disabled`, `readOnly`, `periodicityOnDoubleClick`, `mode`, `filterOption`.

### i18n

- `locale?: Locale` (default English) - override any user-facing string. For weekdays and months you can use ordered arrays or keyed maps. Accessibility labels live under `locale.aria`. Missing keys fall back to the default.

## Example with options

```tsx
import * as React from 'react'
import { Cron, type CronError } from '@/components/cron-builder'

export function ScheduleForm() {
  const [value, setValue] = React.useState('30 5 * * 1-5')
  const [error, setError] = React.useState<CronError>()

  return (
    <Cron
      value={value}
      setValue={setValue}
      mode='multiple'
      clockFormat='24-hour-clock'
      humanizeValue
      shortcuts={['@daily', '@hourly']}
      onError={setError}
    />
  )
}
```

## Localization

```tsx
<Cron
  value={value}
  setValue={setValue}
  locale={{
    everyText: 'ogni',
    prefixHours: 'alle',
    weekDaysMap: {
      sun: 'Domenica',
      mon: 'Lunedi',
      tue: 'Martedi',
      wed: 'Mercoledi',
      thu: 'Giovedi',
      fri: 'Venerdi',
      sat: 'Sabato',
    },
    monthsMap: {
      jan: 'Gennaio',
      feb: 'Febbraio',
      mar: 'Marzo',
      apr: 'Aprile',
      may: 'Maggio',
      jun: 'Giugno',
      jul: 'Luglio',
      aug: 'Agosto',
      sep: 'Settembre',
      oct: 'Ottobre',
      nov: 'Novembre',
      dec: 'Dicembre',
    },
    altWeekDaysMap: {
      sun: 'DOM',
      mon: 'LUN',
      tue: 'MAR',
      wed: 'MER',
      thu: 'GIO',
      fri: 'VEN',
      sat: 'SAB',
    },
    altMonthsMap: {
      jan: 'GEN',
      feb: 'FEB',
      mar: 'MAR',
      apr: 'APR',
      may: 'MAG',
      jun: 'GIU',
      jul: 'LUG',
      aug: 'AGO',
      sep: 'SET',
      oct: 'OTT',
      nov: 'NOV',
      dec: 'DIC',
    },
    errorInvalidCron: 'Cron non valido',
    aria: {
      clearField: 'Cancella campo',
      periodOptions: 'Opzioni del periodo',
      monthsOptions: 'Opzioni dei mesi',
      monthDaysOptions: 'Opzioni dei giorni del mese',
      weekDaysOptions: 'Opzioni dei giorni della settimana',
      hoursOptions: 'Opzioni delle ore',
      minutesOptions: 'Opzioni dei minuti',
    },
  }}
/>
```

## Per-dropdown overrides

```tsx
<Cron
  value={value}
  setValue={setValue}
  dropdownsConfig={{
    minutes: {
      mode: 'single',
      leadingZero: true,
      allowEmpty: 'never',
      filterOption: ({ value }) => Number(value) < 30,
    },
    'week-days': { humanizeLabels: false, allowEmpty: 'never' },
  }}
/>
```
